subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Galileo and the Telescope
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Galileo and the Telescope.

hook.text: In 1609, Galileo pointed a telescope at the sky. What he saw shattered 2,000 years of accepted wisdom.

buildup.text: He found mountains on the Moon, spots on the Sun, and four moons orbiting Jupiter—not everything orbited Earth.

discovery.text: Venus showed phases like the Moon, proving it orbited the Sun. The Ptolemaic model couldn't explain this.

twist.text: The Church put Galileo on trial in 1633. He was forced to recant and spent the rest of his life under house arrest.

climax.text: In 1992—359 years later—the Vatican formally admitted Galileo was right. The apology came three centuries too late.

punchline.text: One telescope, four moons. The Church took 359 years to agree.

quiz.question: What did Galileo's observation of Jupiter's moons prove?
options: Not everything in the sky orbits Earth—some objects orbit other planets
Jupiter was the true center of the solar system
The Moon has no mountains or surface features
